message:
fix(mouse): handle negative coords and track viewport dimensions

- Fix negative coordinate parsing in SGR mouse protocol (use i32, clamp to 0)
  Terminals send negative coords near window edges, causing parse failures
  and escape buffer overflow that leaked chars to Chrome input

- Add getActualViewport() to query Chrome's window.innerWidth/Height
  Chrome may report different dimensions than requested (DPI scaling)

- Track chrome_actual_width/height as source of truth for coordinate mapping
  Update when screencast frame dimensions change (e.g., download bar)

- Add Chrome flags to attempt hiding download bar (limited effect in Chrome 119+)

- Add debug logging for viewport queries and frame dimension changes
